prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `b7cb93ac`.
codex_response:
Implemented `b7cb93ac` end-to-end. The rule is: extract the 4-connected nonzero components, keep the unique largest component in its input orientation, and pack the remaining components into a filled `3x4` rectangle, rotating smaller pieces by `90` degrees when needed.

`arc2_opus46_summary.json` was used as the initial hint. `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` was discarded and corrected in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b7cb93ac/notes.md) because it describes a heuristic template/side-filling story rather than exact rigid packing. No new shared primitive was needed; the task-local shape helpers stayed in [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b7cb93ac/helpers.py).

Verification/build status: official examples passed `4/4`, generated examples passed `1000/1000`, and the artifact files were produced. The generated family stays close to the source task by sampling official-style packed `3x4` motifs, then scattering those components sparsely across a `10 x (10..13)` input grid.
